title: Project Overview
description: Begin with the project README to understand the stroke rehabilitation platform, its user-facing goals, and its full-stack shape. It gives the context needed to connect the client workflows and server features in the following steps.

title: Client Bootstrap
description: The client entry point loads global styling and mounts the application router. This is the best place to see how the browser application begins before following individual screens.
languageLesson: React applications typically mount one root tree; routing then maps URLs to page components without full page reloads.

title: Authentication State
description: The authentication context keeps session state and user actions available across client components. It underpins protected navigation and makes the rehabilitation experience personal to each user.

title: Rehabilitation Dashboard
description: The dashboard brings together the user-facing view of goals and progress. Explore it after the router and authentication context to see how the primary signed-in experience is composed.

title: Goal Setting Flow
description: These goal-setting components guide users through structured SMART-goal capture and confirmation. They illustrate the multi-step client workflow that turns rehabilitation intentions into trackable data.

title: Server Entry Point
description: The server entry point establishes the HTTP application and connects its route modules. Read it next to understand how client submissions reach the backend.

title: Persistent Data Model
description: The database schema defines the persisted records that power users, goals, wellness tracking, and check-ins. This layer gives the server routes a durable model for rehabilitation progress.

title: Route Test Coverage
description: The server route tests demonstrate expected API behavior and protect critical authentication flows during changes. They are a practical companion to the backend entry point and route definitions.

title: Evaluation Automation
description: The evaluation workflow shows how the repository enforces quality checks outside local development. It closes the tour by linking the application architecture to the automated process that guards its baseline.
languageLesson: GitHub Actions workflows express automation as YAML: triggers start jobs, and jobs run ordered steps in isolated runners.
